portal.title: Verify TV power on via remote control
portal.description: Confirm the TV is in standby mode. Press the Power button on the remote. Verify the panel backlight activates and the home screen loads within 15 seconds. Confirm the active application is the Vizio home page.
portal.preconditions:
• TV is in standby (panel backlight off)
• Remote control is functional
portal.detailed_description: With the TV in standby, press the Power key on the remote. Confirm the panel backlight activates within 15 seconds. Press Home and confirm the Home Screen loads with 'APPS & INPUTS' visible. Confirm t
portal.expected_output:
Expected output:

Step 1: Ensure the TV is powered ON before starting the standby cycle.
Expected: TV panel state is ON and device is ready for test actions.

Step 2: Go to the Home screen.
Expected:
portal.detailed_expected_output: Panel backlight turns on within 15 seconds of Power key press. 'APPS & INPUTS' text visible on Home Screen. Active app confirms Vizio Home Page.

candidates.steps_separated:
Step 1: DC Power OFF the TV
  Expected: TV should be powered off
Step 2: Based on the availability of hot keys on the remote, press Watchfree+/Netflix/Prime/Rebox/../ button
  Expected: Respective App launches and Boot2SC countdown timer should not be seen

candidates.title: Verify that Text-to-speech supports System Notification for e.g. (sleep timer will turn off TV)
candidates.preconditions: 1.TV is connected to Network 2.Input at Power On is VIZIO Home or LastUsedTVInput 3.Power Mode: ECO/Quick-Start mode 4.TV is Powered On Note: From FUR9.0 5586L/5586F Spock3 & higher release Power Mode
candidates.steps_separated:
Step 1: Launch Menu by pressing 'Gear/MENU' button on Remote
  Expected: Menu page should get Launched.
Step 2: Select ACCESSIBILITY
  Expected: ACCESSIBILITY page should get Launched
Step 3: Select Talk Back ON
  Expected: Talk back should be ON
Step 4: Navigate >>> System >>> Time >>> Sleep Timer

candidates.title: Verify TV does not power off and no notification displays when audio ceases from an audio app
candidates.preconditions: "Auto Power Off" feature in set to 10min by default.
candidates.steps_separated:
Step 1: Launch an audio app (e.g., Spotify, Pandora) on the TV and start playback.
  Expected: TV launched the respective app content.
Step 2: Stop or pause the audio playback so that no audio is playing, but the app remains open. Wait for more than 10 minutes.
  Expected: The TV remains powered on.

candidates.title: Verify No signal 30 seconds count Down Timer has not displayed when Input Power ON is set to "Last Used TV Input"
candidates.preconditions: 1.TV Powered ON. 2.Network Connected. 3.HDMI devices not connected 4.Input at Power On: Last Used TV Input Note: From FUR9.0 5586L/5586F Spock3 & higher release Power Mode is Removed. By default TV wi
candidates.steps_separated:
Step 1: Press Input button on the remote and select any available HDMI input.
  Expected: No Signal HDMI page is seen.
Step 2: Wait for 30 seconds.
  Expected: 30 seconds Count Down Timer should not display on HDMI-1 Page

candidates.title: Sleep Timer - 60 mins
candidates.preconditions: 1.TV Powered ON and not in OOBE. ​2.TV Connected with Internet. ​Note: From FUR9.0 5586L/5586F Spock3 & higher release Power Mode is Removed. By default TV will be in Eco Mode
candidates.steps_separated:
Step 1: Launch OSD Menu by pressing "Gear/Menu" button from RCU
  Expected: OSD Menu should launch Successfully
Step 2: Select All Settings > System > Sleep Timer > Select 60 minutes
  Expected: 60 minutes timer should be selected.

candidates.title: Verify Sleep Timer in Quick Settings
candidates.preconditions: Network: Wi-Fi Or Ethernet Connected Note: From FUR9.0 5586L/5586F Spock3 & higher release Power Mode is Removed. By default TV will be in Eco Mode.
candidates.steps_separated:
Step 1: Navigate to Sleep Timer in Quick Settings
  Expected: Sleep Timer setting is visible and selectable.
Step 2: Press right or left button on the remote
  Expected: Options to turn off or to set Sleep Timer are displayed.

portal.title: Verify Parental Controls access
portal.description: Navigate to Parental Controls. Verify menu is accessible and PIN protection works.
portal.preconditions:
• TV is powered ON and Settings accessible
• Parental Controls PIN is known (use default if not changed)
portal.detailed_description:
Verify TV display is ON using sysreg check for panel power.
Go to Home screen and confirm APPS & INPUTS is visible.
Open Quick Settings using Menu.
Confirm Quick Settings is open by finding All Settin
portal.expected_output:
Verify TV display is ON using sysreg check for panel power.
Go to Home screen and confirm APPS & INPUTS is visible.
Open Quick Settings using Menu.
Confirm Quick Settings is open by finding All Settin
portal.detailed_expected_output: Parental Controls menu accessible via Settings navigation. PIN entry screen presented. After PIN entry, content restriction settings visible. Rating, Restriction, and Block options confirmed. Back key
